items.title: NASA Artemis II splashes down in Pacific Ocean in ‘perfect’ landing for Moon mission
items.summary: The Integrity craft splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego just after 5:07 p.m. Pacific Time.

items.title: How to watch NASA’s Artemis II splash back down to Earth
items.summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has traveled farther from Earth than any mission before. Here's how to watch the crew's return landing in the Pacific Ocean.

items.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
items.summary: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

items.title: Battery recycler Ascend Elements files for bankruptcy
items.summary: Ascend Elements said that it will be filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in the wake of a canceled government grant and a challenging market for lithium-ion batteries.

items.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
items.summary: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

items.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
items.summary: SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. Expect live demos of humanoid robots, sessions on autonomous driving's software revolution, deep dives into cyber defense and climate tech, and candid conversations about how AI is rewriting the global music and anime industries.

items.title: France to ditch Windows for Linux to reduce reliance on US tech
items.summary: France's move to ditch Windows for Linux is its latest effort to reduce its reliance on American tech giants.

items.title: YouTube Premium and YouTube Music are getting more expensive
items.summary: The YouTube Premium individual plan is increasing from $13.99 to $15.99 per month, while the family plan is increasing from $22.99 to $26.99 per month.

items.title: Every fusion startup that has raised over $100M
items.summary: Fusion startups have raised $7.1 billion to date, with the majority of it going to a handful of companies.

items.title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
items.summary: This is it. Tonight is your last chance to lock in savings of up to $500 for your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. These discounts end at 11:59 p.m. PT.

items.title: PSA: If you use the Meta AI app, your friends will find out and it will be embarrassing
items.summary: Now that Meta released its Muse Spark model, more people are downloading the Meta AI app. And if you join the app, your friends will probably get an Instagram notification about it.

items.title: Snap gets closer to releasing new AI glasses after years-long hiatus
items.summary: Specs has long been teasing the next version of its augmented reality glasses. A new partnership with Qualcomm promises some movement in that department.

items.title: Florida AG to probe OpenAI, alleging possible connection to FSU shooting
items.summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier plans to investigate OpenAI for its alleged harm to minors, potential to threaten national security, and its possible link to a shooting at Florida State University last year.

items.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
items.summary: OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

items.title: EFF is the latest organization to leave X
items.summary: The EFF follows the departure of various news organizations and others who no longer find X a viable source of traffic.

items.title: What founders can learn from Anjuna’s layoffs and recovery
items.summary: In 2021, Anjuna Security was growing fast, hiring aggressively, and chasing a market that seemed limitless. By the end of that year, the venture-backed cybersecurity company had scaled to around 75 employees, building out sales, customer success, and support teams in anticipation of continued hypergrowth. Then 2022 hit.

items.title: Volkswagen drops all-electric ID.4 in the US in pivot back to gas SUVs
items.summary: All resources at its U.S. factory are aimed at the upcoming Atlas SUV.

items.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
items.summary: ChatGPT had reportedly been used to plan the attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University last April. The family of one victim has said that they plan to sue OpenAI over the incident.

items.title: StubHub to pay $10M to settle FTC allegations over ‘deceptive’ ticket pricing
items.summary: The consumer protection agency says the company violated the FTC Act and its Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees by "deceptively" advertising ticket prices on its website without clearly disclosing upfront what the total cost would be, including all mandatory fees.

items.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
items.summary: After falling victim to a hacker, Mercor is facing lawsuits and reportedly losing big-name customers.
